channel.title: rambling on Rohit Goswami
channel.description: Recent content in rambling on Rohit Goswami

item.title: GSoC21 W6: LFortran ASR and Values
item.description: Enriching ASR nodes at compile time Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom GSoC21 W6: LFortran ASR and Values <– You are here!

item.title: GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom
item.description: Project scaffolding and compiler design Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom <– You are here!

item.title: GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs
item.description: Towards the mid-summer evaluation and redirecting efforts Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is one in a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs <– You are here! Overview This week was a bit of a sticky wicket.

item.title: GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards
item.description: Standard practice pragmatic approaches to kind for dftatom Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is the third in a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards <– You are here! Logistics Met with Ondrej on Tuesday Went over my kind implementation Merged older approved MRs Worked on generating tests Talked about the test methodology in general Most of the tests are better off in their integration form (discussed below) Some aspects of the passes may be tested using the doctest setup Set an additional time to discuss the implementation of assumed length character declarations These are not actually used in any dftatom routines but they are very common for utility functions Met with Ondrej on Thursday Discussed repercussions of backends Better, more explicit ASR rules can stem from not relying on the CPP backend Talked about the number of passes (SRC->AST->ASR->LLVM) Started working on getting the right thing happen when faced with character(len=*) Overview This week also saw an increase in community activities on the Fortran discourse, since the J3 meeting is now underway and user polling 1 is in full swing.

item.title: GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling
item.description: Delving into language standards and back-ends for lfortran Background As discussed in a previous post in this series, I have been spending roughly half of each working day with LFortran as part of the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is the second in a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling <– You are here!

item.title: GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff
item.description: Charting paths towards concrete lfortran usage Background As mentioned in an earlier post, I have had the immense pleasure of continuing development of the disruptive lfortran compiler under the aegis of the Fortran Lang organization, financed by the Google Summer of Code and mentored ably by Ondrej Certik. A rather interesting consequence of this is that we are strongly encouraged to write a precis of our activities each week.
